MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Apr-27-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lisa L. Oberg, Esq., and Felicia Feng, Esq., of McKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4175), appearing for defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation in all three cases
Andrew Chew, Esq., and Anne T. Acuna, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Gerald Burr, Sr. (#275287), Frank Delahaye (#275327) and Sherman Fong (#275310)

RULING:
DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corp.'s motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1, 2 and 5 (general negligence, strict liability design and manufacturing defect and punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issues 3 and 4 (false representation and civil battery). Defendant has satisfied its burden of production of making a prima facie case that it is entitled to prevail on its government contractor defense as to non-duty to warn claims and plaintiff has not presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue on this defense. Defendant has satisfied its burden of production that plaintiff does not have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression by clear and convincing evidence and plaintiff has not presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue that he is entitled to punitive damages. Plaintiff represents that he will dismiss his claims for false representation and civil battery. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
